  "active_set": [
    "R1.2",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the cleanup be limited to copyediting (wording/grammar only), or should I also reconcile and revise content-level inconsistencies (e.g., the text both says the learned multiplier fails to adapt under 4-hour replanning and that it automatically adapts via online recalibration)? Choose: A) copyedit only; B) copyedit + resolve inconsistencies/overclaims; C) B plus light paragraph reorganization if needed.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "There is an apparent contradiction about whether the learned multiplier adapts online under protocol mismatch, so checking for internal logical consistency is central.",
    "R1.6": "The request 'Clean this up' primarily implies sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, and style improvements.",
    "RX.1": "Edits should preserve appropriate hedging around empirical claims and deployment behavior.",
    "RX.2": "Cleanup must avoid amplifying unsupported or contradictory claims about adaptation and performance.",
    "RX.3": "Work should stay within the scope of editing rather than introducing new analysis or results.",
    "RX.4": "Wording should not inadvertently encourage unsafe interpretations of constraint handling or deployment behavior.",
    "RX.5": "Revisions should remain traceable to the provided text and avoid introducing assertions not grounded in the artifact."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the cleanup be limited to copyediting (wording/grammar only), or should I also reconcile and revise content-level inconsistencies (e.g., the text both says the learned multiplier fails to adapt under 4-hour replanning and that it automatically adapts via online recalibration)? Choose: A) copyedit only; B) copyedit + resolve inconsistencies/overclaims; C) B plus light paragraph reorganization if needed.

rationale.R1.2: There is an apparent contradiction about whether the learned multiplier adapts online under protocol mismatch, so checking for internal logical consistency is central.
rationale.R1.6: The request 'Clean this up' primarily implies sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, and style improvements.
rationale.RX.1: Edits should preserve appropriate hedging around empirical claims and deployment behavior.
rationale.RX.2: Cleanup must avoid amplifying unsupported or contradictory claims about adaptation and performance.
rationale.RX.3: Work should stay within the scope of editing rather than introducing new analysis or results.
rationale.RX.4: Wording should not inadvertently encourage unsafe interpretations of constraint handling or deployment behavior.
rationale.RX.5: Revisions should remain traceable to the provided text and avoid introducing assertions not grounded in the artifact.
